simulation.name: ΛCDM (abundance matching, no scatter)
simulation.description: Standard NFW halos with abundance-matched baryon fraction

simulation.name: ΛCDM + baryonic feedback (EAGLE-like)
simulation.description: Halo concentration anti-correlates with baryon density (feedback expands halos)

simulation.name: Independent halos (null hypothesis)
simulation.description: Halo params randomized independently of baryons

whyStronger.description: Real baryonic infall contracts the DM halo, creating a tighter density–DM coupling than simple NFW profiles predict.
whyStronger.prediction: High-Σ galaxies should show steeper inner DM profiles than NFW.

whyStronger.description: Stellar feedback in low-Σ dwarfs creates DM cores, boosting f_DM at low density more than NFW expects.
whyStronger.prediction: Dwarf galaxies should show DM cores (not cusps) proportional to star formation history.

whyStronger.description: Our mock uses a deterministic M★–M₂₀₀ relation. Real scatter in this relation could steepen the slope.
whyStronger.prediction: Adding realistic scatter (0.2 dex) to M★–M₂₀₀ should produce slopes closer to observed.

whyStronger.description: In MOND/modified gravity, the transition from Newtonian to modified regime depends on surface density (Σ ∝ a₀/G), naturally creating a steeper f_DM–Σ relation.
whyStronger.prediction: The slope should correlate with a₀ = 1.2×10⁻¹⁰ m/s² and show a characteristic break.

verdict.statement: The baryon–halo coupling is stronger than predicted by ΛCDM, indicating additional physics linking baryonic structure to halo dynamics.
